title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Go Client Systems · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http typed response and failure classifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Status Families
steps.instruction:
Order the Status Families Go HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Status Families.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Status Families assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Status Families, keep Status Families connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go vendor-release candidate observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case G-3. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately. Competency probe: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.text: Order work so Status Families has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
